title: Chicken Stew with Biscuits
description: From Barefoot Contessa Family Style — the ultimate family birthday dinner centerpiece. A rich, creamy chicken and vegetable stew topped with fluffy Parmesan-herb biscuits baked right on top. It's everything you love about chicken pot pie but even better — the biscuits soak up that incredible sauce.

ingredients.item: Carrots, peeled and sliced ½-inch thick

instructions: Preheat the oven to 375°F. Place the chicken breasts on a sheet pan and rub them with olive oil. Sprinkle generously with salt and pepper. Roast for 35 to 40 minutes, or until cooked through. Set aside until cool enough to handle, then cut into large dice. You should have about 4 cups of chicken.

In a large pot or Dutch oven, melt the butter over medium heat. Add the onions, fennel, carrots, and celery and cook over medium-low heat for 8 to 10 minutes, until the onions are translucent. Add the garlic and cook for 1 more minute.

Sprinkle the flour over the vegetables and cook, stirring constantly, for 2 minutes. Add the chicken stock and bring to a boil, stirring constantly. Reduce the heat and simmer for 10 minutes. Add the heavy cream, the diced chicken, peas, parsley, 1 tablespoon salt, and 1½ teaspoons pepper. Mix well and pour into a 10 × 13 × 2-inch baking dish.

Make the biscuits: Combine the flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt in a bowl. Cut in the cold butter using a pastry cutter or your fingers until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Stir in the buttermilk and Parmesan just until combined — don't overmix.
Drop the biscuit dough in large spoonfuls (about 2 tablespoons each) on top of the stew. Bake for 30 to 35 minutes, until the biscuits are golden brown and the stew is bubbling. Serve hot.
tips: The stew can be made up to 2 days ahead and refrigerated. Add the biscuit topping just before baking.
Don't overmix the biscuit dough — a few lumps are fine. Overmixing makes tough biscuits.
The fennel adds a subtle sweetness that makes this stew special — don't skip it.
Source: Barefoot Contessa Family Style by Ina Garten (2002)
